title: Docker Compose Service Port Mappings Validation Bug Fixer

statement.inputFormat: JSON object containing a list of services, each with their own port mappings.
statement.outputFormat: A list of booleans indicating whether each service's port mappings are valid.
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(n), where n is the total number of port mappings across all services.

constraints.description: Total number of port mappings across all services.

examples.explanation: The port mappings are valid for both services.
The port mappings for service2 are invalid due to the duplicate port mapping.

buggyStarterCode.python:
class Solution:
    def validatePortMappings(self, services) -> any:
        # Initialize an empty set to store unique port mappings
        port_set = set()
        # Iterate over each service's port mappings
        for service in services:
            # Iterate over each port mapping
            for port in services[service].ports:
                # Check if the container port is valid (i.e., not negative)
                if port.container_port < 0:
                    # Return false if the container port is invalid
                    return False
                # Check if the host port is valid (i.e., not negative)
                if port.host_port < 0:
                    # Return false if the host port is invalid
                    return False
                # Add the port mapping to the set
                port_set.add(f'{port.container_port},{port.host_port}')
        # Return true if all port mappings are valid
        return True
return Solution().validatePortMappings
